roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: Reduced to 2 milestone(s) from 5 due to time budget (64.0h) relative to demand (265.2h).

vision_profile.current_state: Kiara is a first-year student with no technical skills, seeking an internship and good placement in an IT company.
vision_profile.main_blocker: The biggest obstacle is the lack of foundational coding skills and practical project experience required for entry-level technical roles.
vision_profile.top_motivation: Kiara is highly motivated to secure a good placement and internship in a product-based IT company, aiming to build strong skills early in her academic career.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: The learner can write clean, functional Python code, understand basic algorithms, manage code with Git, and perform fundamental data wrangling tasks, making them ready for entry-level technical internships.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are essential to cover the foundational pillars of programming (Python, Algorithms), professional development (Tools), and initial data handling, which are critical for an AI Foundations Engineer.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a foundational coder, equipped with the essential skills to start building and collaborating on technical projects.

project.title: Automated Data Cleaner for a Startup
project.description: Build a Python script to clean and preprocess a messy CSV dataset from a simulated startup. This involves handling missing values, standardizing formats, and removing duplicates. The final script should be robust and well-documented.
project.deliverable: A Python script, a cleaned CSV file, and a GitHub repository with version control history.
seeded_errors: The provided raw data file contains inconsistent date formats that cause a parsing error, which the learner must identify and fix using Python's datetime module.

modules.title: Python for AI
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are needed to establish a strong base in Python programming, covering syntax, data structures, functions, and error handling, which are fundamental for any AI work.

skills.title: Python Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: Python is the lingua franca of AI and data science; mastering its fundamentals is the first step to building any AI application.

skills.title: Python Data Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Efficiently storing and manipulating data is crucial; understanding lists, tuples, dictionaries, and sets is foundational for data processing.

skills.title: Functions and Modules
skills.why_this_skill: Modularizing code with functions and organizing it into modules improves readability, reusability, and maintainability, essential for larger projects.

skills.title: Error Handling and Debugging
skills.why_this_skill: Robust code anticipates and handles errors gracefully; effective debugging skills are vital for identifying and fixing issues quickly.

skills.title: Basic File I/O
skills.why_this_skill: Interacting with files (reading/writing) is fundamental for data processing, configuration, and logging in almost any application.

science.desc: A Python script for data processing is crashing due to a 'TypeError'. Identify the root cause and implement appropriate error handling.
Production debugging scenario for milestone skills

modules.title: Algorithms & Data Handling
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are chosen to introduce core algorithmic thinking, essential data structures, and basic operations, forming the backbone for efficient problem-solving.

skills.title: Algorithmic Thinking
skills.why_this_skill: Developing a systematic approach to problem-solving is critical for designing efficient and effective code, especially in AI.

scenario.title: Designing a Solution for a New Problem

skills.title: Time and Space Complexity
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding how algorithms scale is crucial for building performant systems, especially with large datasets common in AI.

skills.title: Array Operations
skills.why_this_skill: Arrays are fundamental data structures; efficient manipulation of arrays is key for numerical computing and data processing.

skills.title: String Manipulation
skills.why_this_skill: Text data is ubiquitous in AI; mastering string operations is essential for preprocessing and working with natural language.

skills.title: Basic Searching and Sorting
skills.why_this_skill: Efficiently finding and ordering data is a core computer science skill, directly applicable to optimizing data retrieval and processing in AI.

modules.title: Collaboration & Tools
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to equip the learner with essential development tools and collaborative practices, crucial for working in any professional software environment.

skills.title: Git Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Git is the industry standard for version control; mastering it is non-negotiable for collaborative software development and managing code changes.

skills.title: GitHub Workflow
skills.why_this_skill: GitHub is the most popular platform for hosting Git repositories and collaborating on projects, essential for open-source contributions and team work.

skills.title: Command Line Interface
skills.why_this_skill: Proficiency with the command line is fundamental for developers, enabling efficient navigation, scripting, and interaction with tools and systems.

skills.title: Virtual Environments
skills.why_this_skill: Managing project dependencies with virtual environments prevents conflicts and ensures reproducibility, a best practice in Python development.

skills.title: Code Review Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Participating in code reviews improves code quality, fosters knowledge sharing, and is a standard practice in professional development teams.

scenario.title: Providing Feedback on a Pull Request

science.desc: A team member has submitted a pull request with code that doesn't follow style guidelines. Provide constructive feedback during a code review.

modules.title: Data Wrangling Basics
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are chosen to introduce the learner to the fundamental operations of data manipulation using Pandas, a critical library for data-centric AI tasks.

skills.title: Pandas DataFrames
skills.why_this_skill: Pandas DataFrames are the primary data structure for tabular data in Python, essential for almost any data science or AI project.

skills.title: Data Loading and Saving
skills.why_this_skill: The ability to load data from various sources and save processed data is fundamental for any data pipeline.

scenario.title: Loading Data from a Remote URL

skills.title: Data Cleaning
skills.why_this_skill: Real-world data is often messy; cleaning it is the most time-consuming yet crucial step to ensure reliable analysis and model performance.

skills.title: Data Transformation
skills.why_this_skill: Transforming data into suitable formats is often necessary for analysis or model input, involving operations like filtering, sorting, and creating new features.

skills.title: Basic Data Aggregation
skills.why_this_skill: Summarizing data through aggregation (e.g., mean, sum, count) is fundamental for understanding patterns and preparing data for higher-level analysis.

science.desc: Explain a problem you solved using code and how you approached it, including any challenges you faced and how you overcame them.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: The learner can implement basic machine learning models, preprocess data effectively, and apply object-oriented programming principles to build more structured and maintainable AI applications.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are chosen to transition from foundational programming to core AI development, covering advanced Python, essential math, supervised learning, and data preparation techniques.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving into a Junior AI Developer, capable of building and understanding the core components of machine learning solutions.

project.title: Predictive Model for Customer Churn
project.description: Develop a machine learning model to predict customer churn for a telecom company. This project involves extensive data preprocessing, feature engineering, training a classification model, and evaluating its performance.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook or Python script demonstrating the full ML pipeline (preprocessing, training, evaluation), including a clear explanation of model choices and results.
seeded_errors: The initial dataset contains a significant class imbalance, leading to a model with high accuracy but poor recall for the minority class (churned customers). The learner must identify this and apply appropriate techniques like oversampling or different evaluation metrics.

modules.title: Python for Data & ML
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are selected to deepen Python proficiency, focusing on object-oriented programming and advanced constructs crucial for building scalable and well-structured ML applications.

skills.title: Object-Oriented Programming in Python
skills.why_this_skill: OOP is essential for building modular, reusable, and maintainable codebases, critical for complex AI projects and frameworks.

skills.title: Decorators and Generators
skills.why_this_skill: Advanced Python features like decorators and generators enable more concise, efficient, and Pythonic code, particularly useful for logging, caching, and handling large data streams.

skills.title: Context Managers
skills.why_this_skill: Context managers simplify resource management (e.g., files, network connections), ensuring resources are properly acquired and released, improving code reliability.

skills.title: Advanced File Handling
skills.why_this_skill: Beyond basic I/O, advanced file handling techniques are necessary for working with diverse data formats and managing file systems in complex applications.

skills.title: Advanced Data Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding and utilizing more advanced data structures (e.g., collections module) can lead to more efficient and elegant solutions for specific programming challenges.

scenario.title: Implementing a Least Recently Used (LRU) Cache

science.desc: A data processing script is running slowly due to inefficient data loading. Refactor it using generators to process data in chunks.

modules.title: Math & Stats Essentials
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to provide the fundamental mathematical and statistical understanding necessary to grasp the inner workings of machine learning algorithms.

skills.title: Linear Algebra Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Linear algebra is the language of machine learning; understanding vectors, matrices, and their operations is crucial for comprehending algorithms.

skills.title: Calculus Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding derivatives and gradients is fundamental for grasping how machine learning models learn and optimize their parameters.

skills.title: Probability Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Probability theory provides the framework for understanding uncertainty and making predictions, which is at the heart of many AI algorithms.

skills.title: Descriptive Statistics
skills.why_this_skill: Descriptive statistics allow for summarizing and understanding the main features of a dataset, crucial for exploratory data analysis in ML.

skills.title: Inferential Statistics
skills.why_this_skill: Inferential statistics allows drawing conclusions about populations from samples, crucial for hypothesis testing and understanding model generalization.

science.desc: Describe the process of building a supervised learning model from data acquisition to prediction, highlighting key steps and considerations.

modules.title: Supervised Learning
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are chosen to provide a comprehensive introduction to supervised learning, covering core algorithms, model training, and essential evaluation techniques.

skills.title: Linear Regression
skills.why_this_skill: Linear regression is a foundational algorithm for predicting continuous values, serving as a gateway to more complex models and understanding model interpretability.

skills.title: Logistic Regression
skills.why_this_skill: Logistic regression is a fundamental classification algorithm, widely used for binary prediction tasks and understanding probabilities.

skills.title: Decision Trees
skills.why_this_skill: Decision trees are intuitive and interpretable models, forming the basis for more advanced ensemble methods like Random Forests and Gradient Boosting.

skills.title: Model Evaluation Metrics
skills.why_this_skill: Choosing and interpreting the correct evaluation metrics is crucial for assessing model performance accurately and making informed decisions about model selection.

scenario.title: Choosing Metrics for a Fraud Detection Model

skills.title: Cross-Validation
skills.why_this_skill: Cross-validation is a robust technique for estimating model performance and preventing overfitting, ensuring models generalize well to unseen data.

scenario.title: Evaluating a Model with Limited Data

science.desc: You've trained a classification model that achieves 95% accuracy, but stakeholders report it performs poorly on critical cases. Analyze the evaluation metrics to identify the problem.

modules.title: Feature Engineering & Pipelines
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are chosen to equip the learner with practical techniques for preparing data for machine learning models, including feature transformation and building robust data pipelines.

skills.title: Feature Scaling
skills.why_this_skill: Scaling features is crucial for many ML algorithms to perform optimally, preventing features with larger ranges from dominating the learning process.

skills.title: One-Hot Encoding
skills.why_this_skill: Converting categorical data into a numerical format suitable for machine learning models is a common and essential preprocessing step.

skills.title: Missing Value Imputation
skills.why_this_skill: Effectively handling missing data is crucial to avoid errors and biases in machine learning models, ensuring data integrity.

skills.title: Data Pipelines (Scikit-learn)
skills.why_this_skill: Building robust data pipelines ensures that preprocessing steps are applied consistently and correctly, preventing data leakage and simplifying model deployment.

skills.title: Feature Selection
skills.why_this_skill: Selecting the most relevant features improves model performance, reduces training time, and enhances interpretability, crucial for efficient ML.

scenario.title: Reducing Dimensionality for a Complex Dataset

science.desc: A machine learning model is performing poorly due to irrelevant or redundant features. Apply feature selection techniques to improve its performance.
